Part 1

Getting Started

⏱ About 5 minutes

What is Caelith?

Caelith is a compliance platform built specifically for EU alternative investment fund managers (AIFMs). It replaces the patchwork of spreadsheets, emails, and manual checks that most fund managers use today. In one place you can: manage your fund portfolio, onboard and screen investors against sanctions lists, generate regulatory reports (Annex IV for BaFin/ESMA), check your AIFMD II readiness, and enforce compliance rules — all with a modern dark-themed interface that your compliance team will actually enjoy using.

Logging In

Open your browser and navigate to your Caelith instance. You'll see the login screen:

1
2
3
4
1
E-Mail field — Enter your company email address (e.g. admin@caelith.com)
2
Passwort field — Your password. Click "Zeigen" to reveal it if needed
3
"Angemeldet bleiben" checkbox — Check this to stay logged in across browser sessions
4
"Anmelden" button — Click here to log in. You'll be taken straight to the Dashboard
  1. Enter your email address in the E-Mail field
  2. Enter your password in the "Passwort" (Password) field
  3. Check "Angemeldet bleiben" (Stay Logged In) if you're on a trusted device
  4. Click the "Anmelden" (Log In) button → You'll land on the Dashboard

Your Dashboard at a Glance

After logging in, you'll see the main dashboard — your command center for everything compliance. Let's break down what you're looking at:

1
2
3
4
5
6
7
1
Left sidebar navigation — Access all areas: Dashboard, "Funds" (Funds), "Investors" (Investors), "Regeln & Screening" (Rules & Screening), "Entscheidungen" (Decisions), AIFMD II Check, "Reporte" (Reports). This stays visible on every page.
2
Portfolio overview — Quick stats: 3 "Funds" (funds), 16 "Investors" (investors), 1,890,000 "Anteile" (shares), 3 "Aktionen" (actions) pending. Date shows current reporting period.
3
Compliance Score (60) — Your overall compliance health. Scored out of 100. "Prüfung" (Under Review) indicates items need attention. The trend arrow shows → Stable. Target: get this above 80.
4
Fund cards — Each fund shows its name, type (Spezial_AIF), jurisdiction (DE), capacity %, investor count, and compliance status (color-coded: green = "Konform" (Compliant), orange/red = needs attention).
5
"Aktionswarteschlange" (Action Queue) — Items needing your attention: rejected decisions, medium-severity risk flags. The red badge shows the count.
6
"Fondsgesundheit" (Fund Health) — At-a-glance health for each fund. Green dot = all good, orange = medium risk. Click any fund to dive deeper.
7
Status tabs — Toggle between Status, Risiko, Kalender, and Nachrichten views. Below you'll find Aktive Verstöße (active violations) and KYC/AML Pipeline charts.
The Compliance Copilot button (bottom-right corner with ✨ icon) is available on every page. Click it anytime to ask questions in natural language about your compliance status.
Part 2

Know Your Funds

⏱ About 10 minutes

Viewing Your Fund Portfolio

From the Dashboard, you already see your fund cards (see Part 1, annotation ④). Each card gives you the essentials at a glance:

  • Fund name — e.g. "Hanseatischer Multi-Asset Spezial-AIF"
  • Type & jurisdiction badges — Spezial_AIF, DE
  • Compliance status badge — color-coded (green "Konform", orange "1 med", red for critical issues)
  • Capacity % — How much of the fund's target capacity is filled
  • Investor count — Number of onboarded investors

Click any fund card to open its detail page.

Fund Detail Deep-Dive

The fund detail page is where you manage everything about a specific fund. Here's the Hanseatischer Multi-Asset Spezial-AIF:

1
2
3
4
5
6
1
Fund name & badges — Shows the full fund name with type tags: Spezial_AIF, DE, AIFMD, Aktiv, SFDR Art. 8. These classify your fund for regulatory purposes.
2
Action buttons — Quick access to: "Holdings" (Holdings), "Regelpaket anwenden" (Apply Rule Package), Annex IV XML export, "Evidenz-Paket" (Evidence Package), and "Audit-Paket" (Audit Package). These are your most common fund-level actions.
3
Tab navigation — Seven tabs organize fund data: Übersicht (overview), LMT-Instrumente (liquidity management tools), Auslagerungen (outsourcing), Leitungspersonen (key personnel), Gebühren (fees), Annex IV, "Audit-Paket" (Audit Package).
4
Fund overview section — Shows AIFM name, AIFM LEI, "Auflagedatum" (Inception Date), "Währung" (Currency), and SFDR classification.
5
"PDF exportieren" (Export PDF) — Generate a PDF export of the fund details for offline review or sharing with auditors.
6
"Regulatorische Kennungen" (Regulatory Identifiers) — Your regulatory identifiers: BaFin/CSSF registration number, ISIN, national fund code, MIC code. Click "Bearbeiten" (Edit) to update these.

Understanding Compliance Scores

Compliance scores in Caelith work on a 0–100 scale:

Score RangeStatusWhat It Means
80–100✅ KonformYour fund meets all checked compliance requirements
50–79⚠️ PrüfungSome items need review — check the action queue
0–49🚨 KritischCritical compliance gaps — immediate action required

The score accounts for: KYC completion, sanctions screening status, regulatory filing deadlines, rule violations, and data completeness.

Click on the compliance score circle on the Dashboard to see a breakdown of what's dragging your score down. Fix the highest-impact items first.
Part 3

Managing Investors

⏱ About 10 minutes

Viewing and Filtering Investors

Click "Investors" in the left sidebar. You'll see your complete investor register:

1
2
3
4
5
6
1
Page title — "Investors — Manage investor registten" (Manage your investor register)
2
Action buttons — Three key actions: "Export" (download investor data), "CSV importieren" (CSV Import, for bulk import), and "+ Investor hinzufügen" (+ Add Investor, teal button).
3
Filter bar — Four filters: "Search" (Search by name), "Jurisdiktion" (Jurisdiction/country), "Investorstyp" (Investor Type: Professional/Institutional/Retail), "KYC-Status" (KYC Status: Verified/Pending/Expired). "Zeige 19 von 19 Investors" (Showing 19 of 19 Investors) displays your count.
4
Investor table — Sortable columns: "Name", "Jurisdiktion" (Jurisdiction), "Typ" (Type, color-coded badges), "KYC status", "KYC-Ablauf" (KYC Expiry Date).
5
Investor type badge — "Professionell" (Professional, blue badge) or "Institutionell" (Institutional, purple badge). This determines which regulations and rules apply.
6
KYC status — "Verifiziert" (Verified, green) = KYC complete. "Ausstehend" (Pending, orange) = KYC pending — these investors need attention.

Adding a New Investor

  1. Click the teal "+ Investor hinzufügen" (+ Add Investor) button in the top-right
  2. Fill in the investor's details: name, jurisdiction (country code), investor type
  3. Set the KYC status — typically "Ausstehend" (pending) for new investors
  4. Assign the investor to one or more funds
  5. Click Save → The investor appears in your list

KYC Status Tracking

Every investor has a KYC lifecycle:

StatusColorAction Needed
"Ausstehend" (Pending)OrangeCollect and verify identity documents
"Verifiziert" (Verified)GreenNone — investor is cleared
"Abgelaufen" (Expired)RedRe-verify — KYC documents have expired

CSV Import Workflow

  1. Click "CSV importieren" (CSV Import) on the Investors page
  2. Download the template CSV to see the required column format
  3. Fill in your investor data (name, jurisdiction, type, KYC status, fund assignments)
  4. Upload the CSV file → Caelith validates each row
  5. Review the preview, fix any errors, then confirm the import
CSV imports are additive — they won't delete existing investors. Duplicate detection is based on name + jurisdiction. Always review the preview before confirming.
Part 4

Screen Your Investors Against Sanctions

⏱ About 5 minutes · ⭐ HIGH ROI

Why This Matters

EU fund managers are legally required to screen investors against sanctions lists (EU, UN, OFAC) and PEP (Politically Exposed Persons) databases. Failure to do so can result in:

  • Fines up to €5M+ per violation from BaFin or national regulators
  • Criminal liability for compliance officers
  • License revocation for the AIFM
  • Reputational damage that can destroy your business

Caelith automates this entire process. Here's how:

Loading Sanctions Data

Navigate to Investors → Screening in the left sidebar:

1
2
3
4
1
Page header — "Sanktions- & PEP-Screening" (Sanctions & PEP Screening) — Checks investors against EU/UN/OFAC sanctions lists and PEP databases.
2
"Refresh Lists" button — Click this FIRST to download the latest sanctions data from EU/UN/OFAC. Lists are updated daily by regulators. Always refresh before screening.
3
"Vollständiges Screening starten" button — The big teal button. Screens ALL investors in your system against the loaded sanctions lists. One click, full coverage.
4
Results area — Currently shows "Keine Screening-Ergebnisse" (No Screening Results). After running a screening, results appear here with Clear/Flagged status for each investor.

Running a Full Screening — Step by Step

  1. Click "Refresh Lists" to load the latest EU/UN/OFAC sanctions data
  2. Wait for the refresh to complete (usually 10–30 seconds)
  3. Click "Vollständiges Screening starten" (Run Full Screening)
  4. Caelith checks every investor name against all loaded sanctions and PEP lists
  5. Review results: each investor gets a Clear ✅ or Flagged 🚩 status

Reading Results: Clear vs Flagged

ResultWhat It MeansAction
✅ ClearNo matches foundNone — investor is clean
🚩 FlaggedPotential match foundManual review required (see below)

What to Do with Flagged Matches

  1. Click on the flagged investor to see the match details
  2. Compare: Is it a true match (same person) or a false positive (same name, different person)?
  3. For false positives: Mark as "Resolved — False Positive" with a note explaining why
  4. For true matches: Immediately escalate to your compliance officer and freeze the investor's account
  5. Document your decision in Caelith — this creates an audit trail for regulators
Never ignore a flagged match. Even false positives must be documented. Regulators will check your screening records during audits. Caelith keeps a complete audit trail of every screening run and every resolution decision.
Schedule screening runs at least monthly, or whenever you onboard new investors. Some managers run daily screenings — Caelith handles this easily since it's just one button click.
Part 5

Generate Your AIFMD Annex IV Report

⏱ About 10 minutes · ⭐ HIGH ROI

What is Annex IV and Why You Need It

Annex IV (AIFMD Article 24) is the quarterly/annual regulatory report that every EU AIFM must file with their national regulator (BaFin in Germany) and ESMA. It contains detailed information about your fund's assets, leverage, risk profile, and investor composition. Missing a filing deadline or submitting incorrect data can trigger regulatory sanctions.

Caelith generates this report automatically from your fund data, validates the XML against the official XSD schema, and lets you export it ready for submission.

Step 1: Select Your Fund

Navigate to Reports → Annex IV Report in the left sidebar:

1
2
3
4
5
6
1
Page header — "Annex IV Report" with breadcrumb navigation: Übersicht → Reports → Annex IV Report
2
Export buttons — "Link kopieren" (Copy Link), "XML-Vorschau" (Preview XML), "XML validieren" (Validate XML against XSD), "XML exportieren" (Export/Download XML), "Alle Fonds exportieren" (Export All Funds) (Export All Funds).
3
Fund selector — Choose which fund to generate the report for. Shows fund name, type, and jurisdiction. Set the reporting period with start/end dates.
4
Reporting period — "Startdatum" (Start Date) and "Enddatum" (End Date). Typically quarterly (e.g. 12/31/2025 to 03/30/2026). Make sure these match BaFin's expected filing period.
5
LEI Pre-flight Check — Validates all Legal Entity Identifiers before generating. Click "Run Pre-flight" to check that your AIFM and fund LEIs are valid and active.
6
"AIF-Identifikation" (AIF Identification) — Shows the auto-populated report data: fund name, type, domicile, currency, AIFM name, and LEI. Verify this is correct before exporting.

Step 2: Run LEI Pre-flight Check

  1. On the Annex IV page, find the "LEI Pre-flight Check" section
  2. Click "Run Pre-flight"
  3. Caelith validates your AIFM LEI and fund LEI against the GLEIF database
  4. Green checkmarks = LEIs are valid. Red flags = fix the LEI in your fund settings before proceeding

Step 3: Review the Report Sections

Scroll down the page to review the auto-populated sections:

  • "AIF-Identifikation" (AIF Identification) — Fund name, type, domicile, currency, AIFM, LEI
  • Asset allocation — How the fund's assets are distributed
  • Leverage — Gross and commitment method calculations
  • Risk profile — Market, credit, liquidity, and operational risk
  • Investor concentration — Breakdown by type and jurisdiction

Step 4: Export XML (XSD-Validated!)

  1. Click "XML validieren" (Validate XML) first — this checks the XML against ESMA's official XSD schema
  2. Fix any validation errors that appear (usually missing fields or incorrect formats)
  3. Once validation passes, click "XML exportieren" (Export XML) to download the file
  4. The file is named according to ESMA conventions and ready for upload

Step 5: Submit to BaFin/ESMA

  1. Log in to the BaFin MVP portal (or your national regulator's filing system)
  2. Upload the XML file exported from Caelith
  3. The portal validates and confirms receipt
  4. Save the confirmation reference in Caelith for your audit trail
Use "Alle Fonds exportieren" (Export All Funds) to generate Annex IV reports for all your funds at once. This saves significant time if you manage multiple funds.
Always run "XML validieren" before exporting. Submitting invalid XML to BaFin will result in a rejection, and you may miss the filing deadline.
Part 6

Check Your AIFMD II Readiness

⏱ About 5 minutes

The April 16, 2026 Deadline

AIFMD II (Directive 2024/927/EU) must be transposed into German law via the FoMaStG (Fondsmarktstärkungsgesetz, Fund Market Strengthening Act) by April 16, 2026. This brings significant changes to delegation rules, liquidity management, reporting, and more. Caelith helps you assess how ready you are.

1
2
3
4
5
6
1
"AIFMD II Bereitschaftsbewertung" (AIFMD II Readiness Assessment) — The readiness assessment page. Shows structured gap analysis based on the new directive and FoMaStG draft.
2
Export buttons — "Verlauf" (History of Past Assessments) and ""PDF exportieren" (Export PDF)" (download a PDF report for your board or compliance committee).
3
Countdown & legal disclaimer — "51 Tage verbleibend" (51 Days Remaining). The yellow box warns this is guidance only, not legal advice — consult your compliance officer for a full analysis.
4
Overall score: 16% — The big donut chart shows your current readiness. 16% means significant work remains. The score is calculated from the category scores below.
5
Category breakdown — Each AIFMD II requirement area is scored independently: "Delegation & Auslagerung" (Delegation & Outsourcing, 1/5, 13%), "Liquiditätssteuerung" (Liquidity Management, 1/4, 15%), Reporting & Meldewesen (shown below).
6
"X offen" (X Open) badges — Red badges show how many open items remain in each category. Click a category to see specific action items.

Understanding Your Score

The assessment covers the key AIFMD II requirement areas:

CategoryWhat It Covers
"Delegation & Auslagerung" (Delegation & Outsourcing)New rules on substance requirements, delegation notifications, and oversight
"Liquiditätssteuerung" (Liquidity Management)Mandatory LMT (Liquidity Management Tools) activation and testing
"Reporting & Meldewesen" (Reporting & Regulatory Filing)Enhanced Annex IV reporting, new data fields, increased frequency
Loan OriginationNew rules if your fund originates loans
Investor ProtectionEnhanced disclosure requirements and cost transparency

Closing Compliance Gaps

  1. Click on any category (e.g. "Delegation & Auslagerung") to see specific open items
  2. Each item shows what's required and what's currently missing
  3. Address each item — update your policies, configure settings in Caelith, or document existing compliance
  4. Mark items as complete as you address them → Watch your score rise
  5. Export a PDF for your compliance committee or board using ""PDF exportieren" (Export PDF)"
The 16% score means you're at the beginning. With 51 days remaining, prioritize Delegation & Auslagerung and Liquiditätssteuerung first — these have the most open items and the strictest requirements.
Part 7

Set Up Compliance Rules

⏱ About 10 minutes

Rule Templates — One-Click Compliance

Navigate to Regeln & Screening in the left sidebar. Caelith comes with pre-built rule templates for common regulatory requirements:

1
2
3
4
5
6
1
"Regelwerk" (Rules Engine) — The rules engine page. Integrated and custom compliance rules per asset.
2
"+ Neue Regel" (+ New Rule) button — Create a rule from scratch using the visual editor or JSON format. "Visueller Editor" (Visual Editor) offers a no-code rule builder.
3
Asset selector — "Regeln für Vermögenswert anzeigen" (Show Rules for Asset) — Choose which fund/asset to view or apply rules to. You must select an asset first.
4
"KI-Regel-Generator" (AI Rule Generator) — Describe a compliance rule in plain German and Caelith generates the logic for you. Example: "Privatanleger von SIF-Fonds ausschließen" (exclude retail investors from SIF funds).
5
"Regel generieren" (Generate Rule) button — Click after typing your rule description in plain language. The AI creates the rule logic which you can review and edit before applying.
6
"Schnellvorlagen" (Quick Templates) — Pre-built rules: "Privatanleger sperren" (Block Retail Investors), "€125K Mindestanlage" (min €125K investment), "US-Personen ausschließen" (exclude US persons), "Nur professionelle Anleger" (Professional Investors Only), ""KYC-Verifizierung erforderlich" (KYC Verification Required)", "Nur EU/EWR-Ansässige" (EU/EEA Residents Only).

Using the AI Rule Generator

  1. Select a fund/asset from the dropdown at the top
  2. In the KI-Regel-Generator box, describe your rule in plain German (or English)
  3. Click "Regel generieren" (Generate Rule)
  4. Review the generated rule logic — the AI shows you the conditions and actions
  5. Edit if needed, then click Apply to activate the rule on the selected fund

Example Rule Descriptions:

  • "Privatanleger von SIF-Fonds ausschließen" → Blocks retail investors
  • "Minimum investment of €125,000 for all new investors" → Enforces minimum investment
  • "Do not admit investors from sanctioned countries" → Country-based restriction
  • "KYC muss vor Anteilserwerb abgeschlossen sein" → Requires KYC before share purchase

Applying Rules to Funds

  1. Create or select a rule
  2. Choose which fund(s) it applies to via the asset selector
  3. Set the enforcement mode: Block (prevent violations) or Warn (flag but allow)
  4. Save → The rule is now active and checked automatically
Start with the quick templates. "Privatanleger sperren" and ""KYC-Verifizierung erforderlich" (KYC Verification Required)" are the two most commonly used rules for German Spezial-AIFs.
Part 8

Holdings & Cap Table

⏱ About 5 minutes

Viewing Holdings

Navigate to Funds → Holdings in the left sidebar:

1
2
3
4
1
"Holdings & Cap Table" — View holdings and allocate shares. This is your central view of who owns what in each fund.
2
Action buttons — "CSV importieren" (bulk import holdings from spreadsheet) and "+ Anteile zuweisen" (+ Allocate Shares, teal button).
3
Asset selector — "Asset für Cap Table auswählen" (Select Asset for Cap Table) — Choose which fund to view the cap table for. Required before any data is displayed.
4
Empty state — "Wählen Sie oben ein Asset, um dessen Cap Table anzuzeigen." (Select an asset above to view its cap table.) Select an asset above to see its holdings breakdown.

CSV Import for Holdings

  1. Click "CSV importieren"
  2. Download the template (columns: investor name, share class, number of shares, NAV per share, date)
  3. Fill in your data from your transfer agent or admin spreadsheet
  4. Upload and review the preview
  5. Confirm → Holdings are imported and the cap table updates

Share Allocation Tracking

  1. Select a fund from the asset dropdown
  2. Click "+ Anteile zuweisen" (+ Allocate Shares)
  3. Select the investor, share class, number of shares, and effective date
  4. Confirm the allocation → Cap table updates with the new holding
Keep your cap table updated in Caelith — it feeds directly into the Annex IV report's investor concentration section and compliance rule checks.
Part 9

Configure Your Platform

⏱ About 5 minutes

Settings Overview

Click your profile name at the bottom of the sidebar, or navigate to "Settings":

1
2
3
4
5
1
Settings header — "Settings — Manage accountn" (Manage your account).
2
Konto section — Profil (your profile), Darstellung (appearance/theme), Organisation (company settings).
3
Compliance section — Regulatorik (regulatory settings), Screening (sanctions screening configuration), Benachrichtigungen (notification preferences).
4
System section — Sicherheit (security/2FA), Datenverwaltung (data management), and the red "Danger Zone" for destructive actions.
5
Profile details — Your name, email, role (Admin), job title (CEO), and phone number for 2FA and compliance notifications.

Organization Setup

  1. Go to "Organisation" (Organization) in the settings sidebar
  2. Enter your company name, legal entity form, and registered address
  3. Add your company's LEI (Legal Entity Identifier)
  4. Set the default jurisdiction and regulatory regime (AIFMD, KAGB)

Regulatory Configuration

  1. Go to "Regulatorik" (Regulatory Settings)
  2. Select your primary regulator (BaFin, CSSF, FMA, etc.)
  3. Configure reporting periods (quarterly/annually)
  4. Set filing deadline reminders

Screening Settings

  1. Go to "Screening"
  2. Choose which sanctions lists to include (EU, UN, OFAC — recommend all three)
  3. Set the fuzzy match threshold (higher = more false positives but fewer missed matches)
  4. Configure automatic screening triggers (on investor onboarding, scheduled, etc.)
Set up 2FA in the "Sicherheit" (Security) section. Compliance platforms should always use multi-factor authentication.
Part 10

EMT / EET / EPT Templates

⏱ About 5 minutes

What They Are and Who Needs Them

These are standardized data exchange templates required by MiFID II and the PRIIPs regulation:

TemplateFull NamePurpose
EMTEuropean MiFID TemplateProduct governance data for distributors — target market, costs, risks
EETEuropean ESG TemplateSustainability-related disclosures (SFDR Article 6/8/9 data)
EPTEuropean PRIIPs TemplateKey Information Document (KID) data for packaged investment products

If your funds are distributed to retail or semi-professional investors through banks or platforms, you'll need these.

How to Generate

EMT/EET/EPT generation is currently available via the Caelith API:

EndpointDescription
GET /api/reports/emt/:fundIdGenerate EMT for a specific fund
GET /api/reports/eet/:fundIdGenerate EET for a specific fund
GET /api/reports/ept/:fundIdGenerate EPT for a specific fund

Auto-Filled vs Manual Fields

Auto-filled from your Caelith data: Fund name, ISIN, LEI, SFDR classification, fee structure, risk indicators, target market (based on investor type settings).

Manual fields you'll need to provide: Specific cost breakdowns (if not entered in fund fees), benchmark details, performance scenarios, and any custom distribution restrictions.

A full UI for EMT/EET/EPT generation is coming soon. For now, use the API endpoints or ask the Compliance Copilot to help you generate them.
Part 11

Compliance Copilot

⏱ About 5 minutes

What It Can Answer

The Compliance Copilot (the ✨ button in the bottom-right corner of every page) is an AI assistant that understands your fund data and regulatory requirements. It can:

  • Answer questions about your compliance status ("Are any investors missing KYC?")
  • Explain regulatory requirements ("What does AIFMD II require for delegation?")
  • Help with report generation ("Generate the Annex IV summary for Fund X")
  • Guide you through workflows ("How do I screen a new investor?")
  • Analyze your data ("Which fund has the lowest compliance score?")

Example Queries

You AskCopilot Returns
"Which investors have pending KYC?"List of investors with "Ausstehend" (Pending) status, grouped by fund
"When is the next Annex IV filing due?"Filing deadline based on your regulatory calendar
"What's our AIFMD II readiness for delegation?""Delegation & Auslagerung" (Delegation & Outsourcing) score with specific open items
"Create a rule to block US persons"Generates a compliance rule you can apply to any fund
"Summarize our sanctions screening results"Overview of last screening: X clear, Y flagged, Z pending review

Tips for Best Results

  • Be specific — "KYC status for Hanseatischer fund" beats "show me KYC"
  • Use fund names — The Copilot knows your exact fund names
  • Ask in German or English — Both work equally well
  • Ask follow-ups — "Why is it only 16%?" after asking about AIFMD II readiness
  • Request actions — "Export this as a table" or "Create a rule for this"

Keyboard Shortcuts

ShortcutAction
⌘K / Ctrl+KOpen global search
G then DGo to Dashboard
G then IGo to Investors
G then FGo to Funds
G then RGo to Rules
G then SGo to Settings

API Endpoints

EndpointMethodDescription
/api/fundsGETList all funds
/api/funds/:idGETFund details
/api/investorsGETList all investors
/api/investorsPOSTCreate investor
/api/screening/runPOSTRun sanctions screening
/api/screening/refreshPOSTRefresh sanctions lists
/api/reports/annex-iv/:fundIdGETGenerate Annex IV XML
/api/reports/annex-iv/validate/:fundIdPOSTValidate Annex IV XML

/api/reports/eet/:fundIdGETGenerate EET
/api/reports/ept/:fundIdGETGenerate EPT
/api/aifmd-checkGETAIFMD II readiness score
/api/rulesGET/POSTList/create compliance rules
/api/holdings/:fundIdGETFund holdings/cap table

Glossary of Regulatory Terms

TermMeaning
AIFMAlternative Investment Fund Manager — the licensed entity managing the fund
AIFMDAlternative Investment Fund Managers Directive (2011/61/EU) — the EU regulation governing AIFMs
AIFMD IIAmending directive (2024/927/EU) — major update effective April 2026
Annex IVThe standardized regulatory report filed quarterly/annually with national regulators and ESMA
BaFinBundesanstalt für Finanzdienstleistungsaufsicht — Germany's financial regulator
CSSFCommission de Surveillance du Secteur Financier — Luxembourg's financial regulator
EMTEuropean MiFID Template — product governance data for distribution
EETEuropean ESG Template — sustainability disclosure data
EPTEuropean PRIIPs Template — KID data for packaged products
ESMAEuropean Securities and Markets Authority — EU-level regulator
FoMaStGFondsmarktstärkungsgesetz — German law transposing AIFMD II
KAGBKapitalanlagegesetzbuch — Germany's Investment Code
KYCKnow Your Customer — identity verification process for investors
LEILegal Entity Identifier — 20-character global ID for legal entities
LMTLiquidity Management Tools — mechanisms to manage fund liquidity (gates, swing pricing, etc.)
PEPPolitically Exposed Person — individuals with prominent public functions
SFDRSustainable Finance Disclosure Regulation — EU ESG disclosure framework
Spezial-AIFSpecial AIF — German fund type restricted to (semi-)professional investors
XSDXML Schema Definition — the validation format for Annex IV XML files
